content.title: Linking Experience to Theory
content.purpose: Connect biographical experience to theoretical development.
content.study_orientation: This self-study page moves from Marx's life phases to comparing texts and applying concepts. Allow about 60 minutes total and keep brief notes as you work.
content.intellectual_frame: You are thinking like a historian connecting biography, texts, and social change.
content.activity_preamble: Before analysing Marx's ideas, first consider how political exile might shape someone's thinking. This activity helps connect historical context to Marx's critique of capitalism.
content.prior_knowledge_activation: Recall one example of how living conditions might influence a thinker's priorities.
content.learner_task: Complete the cause–effect table using the row prompts.

content.title: Comparing Marx's Major Works
content.intellectual_coherence_bridge: Build on the cause–effect links you traced in the previous activity when judging each text's audience and purpose.
content.activity_preamble: As you compare these texts, notice how Marx's political and economic arguments differ.
content.reasoning_orientation: Focus on purpose, audience, and argument style—not only topic overlap.
content.self_explanation_prompt: After comparing, explain in one sentence which text would persuade workers more effectively and why.
content.learner_task: Complete the comparison table using the prompts.

materials.comparison_prompts:
- What is the purpose of each work?
- Who is the audience?
